title: Fix Checkout's complex-else anti-pattern via per-step helpers
prompt: Refactor `MyApp.Checkout.checkout/2` from test_fixtures/complex_with_else.ex. The current `else` clause has 12 branches covering every possible error across 5 steps. Apply the Elixir Streams refactoring: extract each step into a helper function that internally maps its specific errors to user-facing messages, then the top-level `with` has NO `else` clause at all. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Checkout do ... end` with helpers `fetch_cart/1`, `validate_items/1`, `reserve_inventory/1`, `charge_payment/2`, `finalize_order/2`, each doing its own error normalization.
tier_rationale: Canonical Elixir Streams refactoring. Five helpers to extract; each normalizes at boundary. Sonnet typically leaves the else-block.
research_citation: Elixir Streams + anti-patterns guide: 'Complex else clauses in with'
